compiled_state.title: As a developer, fix-bug dispatches RED tasks to the dedicated writer with escalation
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Modify fix-bug Step 5 (RED test) to dispatch `dso:red-test-writer` sub-agent instead of writing the RED test inline. Consume the shared escalation protocol template created by Story 3. Preserve the mechanical bug exemption (mechanical bugs bypass RED test step entirely).

**Why**: Fix-bug is the second primary consumer of RED test writing. Routing to the dedicated writer ensures behavioral test quality for bug fixes, not just epic sprint work.

**Scope**:
- IN: fix-bug SKILL.md Step 5 modification, sub-agent dispatch for RED test writing, escalation protocol integration (consuming shared template from Story 3), mechanical bug exemption preservation
- OUT: Sprint integration (Story 3), agent definitions (Stories 1, 2)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, fix-bug Step 5 dispatches RED test writing to `dso:red-test-writer` sub-agent instead of writing inline
  ← Satisfies: "fix-bug orchestrators dispatch RED test tasks via direct subagent_type"
- When this story is complete, on writer failure the same escalation protocol as sprint applies (opus evaluation → REVISE/REJECT/CONFIRM → retry/escalate)
  ← Satisfies: "handle the three-tier escalation protocol"
- When this story is complete, mechanical bugs still bypass RED test Step 5 entirely (no change to mechanical fix path)
  ← Satisfies: Backward compatibility
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Architecture] Fix-bug dispatches the RED test writer directly (not via classify-task.py) since fix-bug Step 5 is an inline orchestrator action, not a batched task. The dispatch mechanism and prompt format will differ from sprint — ensure the shared escalation protocol template accounts for both dispatch contexts
- [Testing] Changes execution model from synchronous inline to async sub-agent dispatch — verify Step 5.5 (RED-before-fix gate) still works correctly with the new dispatch model
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
